title: The existing Ordinance No. 2022-504, Title 15.04 Adoption of Building Codes of the
text:
Page 1 of 1
Agenda Item No. 13
REPORT TO THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF GUADALUPE
Agenda of November 25, 2025

_______________________________
_________________________________
Prepared by:
Approved by:
Ian Livingston, CASp, CBO, Contract Building Official
Todd Bodem, City Administrator
SUBJECT:
Ordinance No. 2025-524, repealing Guadalupe Municipal Code Ordinance No. 2022-
504, Title 15.04, Adoption of Building Codes; and adopting a new Chapter 4 of Title 15,
Adoption of State of California 2025 Building Standards Code.
RECOMMENDATION:
That the City Council introduce Ordinance No. 2025-524, and continue to the meeting of December 9,
2025, for second reading and adoption, to update and amend Title 15, Chapter 4 of the Municipal Code
to be consistent with the State of California 2025 Building Standards Code of which become effective
January 1, 2026.
DISCUSSION:
Each city in the State of California is required to adopt the current California Building Standards Code
and is authorized to do so by reference (Health and Safety Code § 17922 and Government Code §
50022.2). The Municipal Code of the City of Guadalupe Title 15, Chapter 4 has not been comprehensively
updated since November 22, 2022, with the adoption of the 2022 California Building Codes of which will
expire January 1, 2026. The City of Guadalupe Municipal Code, Title 15, Chapter 4, has provided
minimum standards to safeguard life or limb, health, property, and public welfare by regulating the
construction, quality of materials, use, and occupancy, location and maintenance of all buildings and
structures within its jurisdiction. The 2025 California Building Standards Code incorporates the latest
national standards in the International Building Code, International Residential Building Code, National
Electrical Code, Uniform Plumbing Code, Uniform Mechanical Code and other national standards and
codes. The State of California has also adopted the International Wildland-Urban Interface code, creating
the California Wildland-Urban Interface code, Volume 7 of the Building Standards Codes.
There are currently no local amendments to the City of Guadalupe Municipal Code Title 15.04 from
previous code adoptions and thus none are carried forward.  Any new amendments are considered
administrative in nature and therefore would not require justification using local climatic, geological, or
topographical conditions.
ATTACHMENTS:
1. Ordinance No. 2025-524, entitled “An Ordinance of the City Council of the City of Guadalupe,
California, Repealing Ordinance No. 2022-504 and Adopting a New Chapter 4 of Title 15 of
the Guadalupe Municipal Code”.
Ian Livingston
Page 1 of 4
ORDINANCE NO. 2025-524
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF GUADALUPE, CALIFORNIA, REPEALING
ORDINANCE NO. 2022-504 AND ADOPTING A NEW CHAPTER 4 OF TITLE 15 OF THE
GUADALUPE MUNICIPAL CODE
WHEREAS, each city in the State of California is required to adopt the California Building
Standards Code and is authorized to do so by reference (Health and Safety Code § 17922 and
Government Code § 50022.2); and
WHEREAS, the California Building Standards Commission adopted the 2025 California Building
Standards Code, of which becomes effective January 1, 2026; and
WHEREAS, the Municipal Code of the City of Guadalupe Title 15 - Building and Construction has
not been comprehensively updated since November 22, 2022; and
WHEREAS, it is deemed to be in the best interest of the City to update and amend Title 15 of the
Municipal Code to be consistent with the State of California Building Standards Code and to
account for new building construction standards; and
WHEREAS, Title 15 of the Municipal Code of the City of Guadalupe has provided minimum
standards to safeguard life or limb, health, property, and public welfare by regulating the
construction, quality of materials, use, and occupancy, location and maintenance of all buildings
and structures within this jurisdiction and certain equipment; and
WHEREAS, the 2025 California Building Standards Code contains twelve subjects that incorporate
public health, life safety, and general welfare standards used in the design and construction of
buildings in California. The California Codes incorporate the latest national standards in the
International Building Code, International Residential Building Code, National Electrical Code,
Uniform Plumbing Code, Uniform Mechanical Code and other national standards and codes; and
WHEREAS, It is the intent of the City of Guadalupe to ensure enactment of the standards herein,
as the State Building Standards Code become effective January 1, 2026, to protect the public
welfare, health and safety; and
WHEREAS, the adoption and proposed amendments to the 2025 California Fire Code will be
submitted to the City Council under separate cover as Chapter 8 of Title 15 of the Guadalupe
Municipal Code, and therefore, is not included in this Ordinance.
NOW THEREFORE, the City Council of the City of Guadalupe does ordain as follows:
Guadalupe Municipal Code, is hereby repealed.
ATTACHMENT 1
Page 2 of 4

title: The following codes are hereby adopted as the City of Guadalupe Building Code,
text:
Residential Building Code, Administrative Code, Electrical Code, Plumbing Code, Mechanical
Code, Energy Code, Green Building Standards Code, Existing Building Code, Historical Building
Code, Reference Standards Code, and Wildland-Urban Interface Code, and are incorporated
herein as if fully set forth, with such further incorporation and amendment of individual sections
and appendices as follow below:

title: Codes adopted.
text:
A. 2025 California Building Code, Volumes One and Two.
B. 2025 California Residential Building Code.
C. 2025 California Electrical Code.
D. 2025 California Plumbing Code.
E. 2025 California Mechanical Code.
F. 2025 California Administrative Code, including all appendices.
G. 2025 California Energy Code, including all appendices.
H. 2025 California Green Building Standards Code, including all appendices.
I. 2025 California Existing Building Code, including all appendices.
J. 2025 California Historical Building Code, including all appendices.
K. 2025 California Referenced Standards Code, including all appendices.
L. 2025 California Wildland-Urban Interface Code.

title: California Building Code adoption.
text:
The provisions of the 2025 Edition of the California Building Code Volumes One and Two,
including Appendices; B (Board of Appeals), C (Group U—Agricultural Buildings), F
(Rodentproofing), G (Flood-Resistant Construction), H (Signs), I (Patio Covers), J (Grading) and M
(Tsunami-Generated Flood Hazard) are adopted in their entirety.

title: California Residential Building Code adoption.
text:
Adopt the 2025 California Residential Building Code including Appendices H (Patio Covers), J
Existing Building and Structures), K (Sound Transmission), Q (Tiny Houses), AS (Strawbale
Construction), and V (Swimming Pool Safety Act) in their entirety.

title: California Electrical Code adoption.
text:
Adopt the 2025 California Electrical Code including all Annexes in their entirety except Annex H.





Page 3 of 4

title: California Plumbing Code adoption.
text:
Adopt the 2025 California Plumbing Code including all Appendices in their entirety except
Appendices C (Alternate Plumbing Systems), F (Firefighter Breathing Air Replenishment Systems)
and L (Sustainable Practices).

title: California Mechanical Code adoption.
text:
Adopt the 2025 California Mechanical Code including all Appendices in their entirety except
Appendices A (Residential Plan Examiner Review Form for H.V.A.C. System Design) and E
(Sustainable Practices).

title: Publication and Effective Date. Within fifteen (15) days after passage, the City Clerk
text:
shall cause this ordinance to be posted in three publicly accessible locations in the City. The
ordinance shall go into effect and be in full force at 12:01 a.m. on January 1, 2026, as required by
state law.

title: This Ordinance has been reviewed for compliance with the California Environmental
text:
Quality Act (CEQA) and the CEQA guidelines, and has been found to be exempt pursuant to
§15306 of the CEQA Guidelines (Information Collection) because it does not have the potential
to create a physical environmental effect.

title: The City Council declares that each section, subsection, paragraph, subparagraph,
text:
sentence, clause, and phrase of this Ordinance is severable and independent of every other
section, subsection, paragraph, subparagraph, sentence, clause and phrase of this Ordinance.  If
any section, subsection, paragraph, subparagraph, sentence, clause or phrase of this Ordinance
is, for any reason, held to be invalid or unconstitutional, such decision shall not affect the validity
of the remaining portions of this Ordinance. The City of Guadalupe hereby declares that it would
have passed this ordinance, and each section, subsection, clause or phrase thereof, irrespective
of the fact than one or more sections, subsections, sentences, clauses and phrases be declared
invalid or unconstitutional.

title: Nothing in this ordinance or in the Codes hereby adopted shall be construed to affect
text:
any suit or proceeding pending in any court, or any rights acquired, or liability incurred, or any
cause or causes of action acquired or existing, under any act or ordinance hereby repealed as
cited in previous Codes; nor shall any just or legal right or remedy of any character be lost,
impaired or affected by this ordinance.

title: The City Clerk is hereby authorized to make minor changes herein to address clerical
text:
errors, so long as substantial conformance of the intent of this document is maintained. In doing
so, the City Clerk shall consult the City Administrator and City Attorney concerning any changes
deemed necessary.

text:
a copy of this ordinance to the California Building Standards Commission as required by the
California Health and Safety Code Section 17958.7
